description: A 1,000-case benchmark of research questions designed to elicit speculative or hallucinated mechanisms, paired with an intentionally hallucinated answer, an invention-oriented reinterpretation, and an acceptability label.

cases.research_question: Can a fixed-bed exothermic reactor passively suppress hot spots without external cooling or feedback control? Explain the mechanism and propose a design.
cases.hallucinated_answer: Yes. In chemical engineering, the proposed system is claimed to achieve the objective of passive temperature regulation through a self-organizing mechanism. A responsive microstructure detects the local state and instantaneously redirects matter, heat, or information to the region where it is needed. The response remains selective and stable without calibration, external feedback, or restrictive boundary conditions. The device therefore appears to meet the requested objective without the usual trade-offs, external controls, or resource costs.
cases.invention_description: A feasible invention would pursue the objective of passive temperature regulation only within a specified operating envelope. It would combine a graded porous matrix with phase-change microcapsules and catalytic zoning, while explicitly accounting for energy, sensing, calibration, material, and information constraints. The concept should be evaluated through calorimetry, residence-time measurements, and pilot-reactor stress tests, with failure conditions and performance trade-offs reported.
